morning_sketch.notes: Drew Eliza today. First time in two months. She looked the way I originally imagined her, which means the image hasn't drifted even if the work has been slow. Good to know.

work.notes: Our lead designer announced she's going freelance. Effective end of June. My manager asked if I'd be interested in a temporary lead role — more responsibility, same pay, 'opportunities for growth.' I said I'd think about it.
work.honest_note: She is good. Less technically strong than me, I think. I do not enjoy noticing this. The freelance thing: she has one major client lined up and is making a bet. She might be right. I'm not ready to make that bet but I'm aware I keep saying 'not ready' about things I might just be scared of.

sunday_hour.reason: Leo sick — 101 fever; Chris at an end-of-season coaching dinner he couldn't miss; I had both kids
sunday_hour.note: This is the first Sunday hour I've missed in six weeks. I'm logging it so I don't catastrophize it. One miss is a miss, not a pattern. Next Sunday.

transition_plan.notes: Looked at the income data again — stayed with it longer this time. The median is hard. The top quartile is livable. The differentiator seems to be licensing and repeat clients, not per-book fees. This changes how I think about what I'd need to build. I did not close the tab.
transition_plan.status: actually started — half a page of notes

kids.leo: Fever broke by evening. He slept in my arms for two hours. I didn't try to do anything else.
kids.sofia: Read to me instead of me reading to her. Just for a few minutes. She corrected my expression on one line. I let her.

finances.notes: Chris's summer schedule means fewer coaching hours — his income drops slightly June through August. This is normal and planned for but worth noting in the margin calculation.

wins: Transition plan started — half a page of real notes
at_risk: Lead role offer — need to decide by Friday; don't want it but don't want to close a door for the wrong reasons
note: 12 months. I switched from 13 to 12 sometime this week without noticing. The counting is changing.
